lessons.title: ClickUp assignees silently ignored on PUT
lessons.pattern: Task created with 200 OK but assignees are empty in ClickUp UI
lessons.root_cause: PUT /api/v2/task/{id} ignores the assignees field entirely. Also: wrong format [{'id': 123}] used instead of flat int list [123].
lessons.fix: Set assignees ONLY at creation time via POST /api/v2/list/{list_id}/task. Use flat integer list: [100842373], not [{'id': 100842373}].
lessons.prevention: See clickup-ops SKILL.md API Rules section. Always use create_task() canonical template.

lessons.title: ClickUp status not found error on task creation
lessons.root_cause: Status string in payload doesn't match the list's exact configured status names. E.g. sent 'to do' when list uses 'todo'.
lessons.fix: Always fetch the list first (GET /api/v2/list/{id}) and read the exact status strings from the 'statuses' array before creating tasks.
lessons.prevention: See clickup-ops SKILL.md Rule 5. Run _check_statuses script before any bulk task creation.

lessons.title: ClickUp subtask creation 404 via /subtask endpoint
lessons.root_cause: The /subtask endpoint does not exist in this ClickUp workspace configuration.
lessons.fix: Create subtasks via POST /api/v2/list/{list_id}/task with 'parent': parent_task_id in the payload.
lessons.prevention: See clickup-ops SKILL.md Rule 6 and create_subtask() canonical template.

lessons.title: ClickUp due dates off by one day in IST
lessons.pattern: Task created for Jun 9 appears as Jun 8 in ClickUp
lessons.root_cause: datetime(yyyy, mm, dd).timestamp() uses midnight UTC, which is 05:30 IST next day — ClickUp displays the UTC date.
lessons.fix: Use datetime(yyyy, mm, dd, 18, 0).timestamp() * 1000 and set due_date_time: False. 18:00 local safely falls on the correct calendar date in any timezone.
lessons.prevention: Always use the ms() helper: def ms(y,m,d): return int(datetime(y,m,d,18,0).timestamp()*1000)

lessons.title: Morning report capacity default was 40h — corrected to 48h
lessons.pattern: Weekly capacity of 40h underestimated actual work hours, causing false OVERLOADED flags and inflated alarm in reports.
lessons.root_cause: Default capacity was set to 40h in three places: hr_structure.json (21 people), generate_morning_report.py (6 fallback lines), and SKILL.md documentation.
lessons.fix: Updated all occurrences: hr_structure.json capacity_hours_per_week and available_hours_per_week 40→48; Python script fallbacks 40→48; SKILL.md docs 40h→48h.
lessons.prevention: When adjusting company-wide defaults, change all three layers: (1) hr_structure.json source of truth, (2) script fallback defaults, (3) SKILL.md documentation. Use grep to verify zero stale references remain.

lessons.title: python-dotenv load_dotenv() crashes in CommandCenter runtime
lessons.pattern: `from dotenv import load_dotenv; load_dotenv()` raises `AssertionError` — frame.f_back is None when the agent runs via subprocess or piped context.
lessons.root_cause: load_dotenv() inspects the caller's stack frame to locate the .env file. In the CommandCenter runtime, scripts are invoked via subprocess.run() or from within the MAF framework where there is no Python caller frame.
lessons.fix: Replace all `from dotenv import load_dotenv; load_dotenv()` with a manual .env parser. Created `.tmp/scripts/load_env.py` — canonical shared loader: reads AGENT_DIR/.env line-by-line, parses KEY=VALUE, sets os.environ with setdefault semantics. Import as: `from load_env import load_env; load_env()`.
lessons.prevention: Never use python-dotenv in any agent script. Use `from load_env import load_env; load_env()` instead. Removed python-dotenv from pyproject.toml dependencies. All 16 scripts that used load_dotenv() were migrated to the new pattern on 2026-06-23.

lessons.title: No bulk task status update tooling — mass operations were done one-by-one
lessons.pattern: Closing 14 tasks for a departing employee required 14 individual PUT /task/{id} calls, each with manual .env parsing. Slow and error-prone.
lessons.root_cause: No shared script existed for bulk status transitions. The clickup-ops skill only had create_task, create_subtask, and update_task as individual operations.
lessons.fix: Created `.github/skills/clickup-ops/scripts/bulk_update_tasks.py` — supports: close all tasks by assignee (--assignee), close specific task IDs (--task-ids), close from plan JSON (--plan). Has --dry-run mode. Handles subtasks automatically.
lessons.prevention: When mass mutation is needed: `python .github/skills/clickup-ops/scripts/bulk_update_tasks.py --assignee <ID> --to-status Closed --dry-run` first, then re-run without --dry-run after review.

lessons.title: No cross-list unassigned task search — finding work for departing employee required manual space-by-space scanning
lessons.pattern: Searching for unassigned tasks matching Raja's skills (Python, Django, Flask, Electron) required fetching tasks from 7+ lists individually, grep/filter manually. Took many turns and hit rate limits.
lessons.root_cause: All task fetching was list-scoped. No tool existed to enumerate unassigned tasks across the whole workspace with skill-keyword matching.
lessons.fix: Created `.github/skills/clickup-ops/scripts/find_unassigned_tasks.py` — enumerates all spaces/folders/lists, finds tasks with empty assignees, optionally filters by skill keywords. Supports --space-id scoping, --skip-status exclusion, --json output.
lessons.prevention: `python .github/skills/clickup-ops/scripts/find_unassigned_tasks.py --skills 'Python,Django,Flask'` for skill-matching. Use `--json` for structured output readable by other scripts.
